scenes.title: How a Compiler Works
scenes.subtitle: Turning human-readable code into machine instructions
scenes.label: Scene 1 / 9
stepSubtitles: Presenting the Quick Bytes Interactive Pipeline Visualizer.
Translating source code to machine execution.
Press play or next to begin the walkthrough.

scenes.title: Source Code
scenes.subtitle: A programmer writes high-level statements in languages like C++, Java, or Rust.
scenes.label: Scene 2 / 9
stepSubtitles: A programmer writes a C++ function to add two integers.

scenes.title: Compiler Pipeline Overview
scenes.subtitle: Information flows through a structured, multi-stage machine.
scenes.label: Scene 3 / 9
stepSubtitles: Source: Reading raw C++ source files from disk.
Lexer: Grouping character sequences into lexical tokens.
Parser: Building the hierarchical Abstract Syntax Tree (AST).
Semantic: Checking type safety, scope, and variable definitions.
Optimization: Modifying code to run faster and use less memory.
CodeGen: Translating optimized structures into target assembly.
Machine: Writing binary instructions to the CPU executable memory.
Overview complete. Let's explore each phase in detail.

scenes.title: Lexical Analysis
scenes.subtitle: Convert raw characters into structured tokens.
scenes.label: Scene 4 / 9
stepSubtitles: Loading the input stream characters into lexical analysis phase.
Tokenizing 'int' keyword (type specifier).
Tokenizing 'add' identifier (function name).
Tokenizing '(' punctuation (parameter list start).
Tokenizing 'int' keyword (parameter type).
Tokenizing 'a' identifier (parameter name).
Tokenizing ',' punctuation (parameter separator).
Tokenizing 'int' keyword (parameter type).
Tokenizing 'b' identifier (parameter name).
Tokenizing ')' punctuation (parameter list end).
Tokenizing '{' punctuation (function body block start).
Tokenizing 'return' keyword (control flow statement).
Tokenizing 'a' identifier (variable expression).
Tokenizing '+' operator (binary arithmetic).
Tokenizing 'b' identifier (variable expression).
Tokenizing ';' punctuation (statement terminator).
Tokenizing '}' punctuation (function body block end).

scenes.title: Parsing
scenes.subtitle: Build an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) to define program structure.
scenes.label: Scene 5 / 9
stepSubtitles: Instantiating the root 'Program' node of the tree.
Parsing the function definition node for 'add'.
Parsing the function body block containing return statement.
Parsing the binary operator '+' expression.
Parsing parameter 'a' reference node.
Parsing parameter 'b' reference node.
Linking Program root to the function definition node.
Linking function definition node to return statement.
Linking return statement to binary addition operator.
Linking addition operator left operand to parameter 'a'.
Linking addition operator right operand to parameter 'b'.

scenes.title: Semantic Analysis
scenes.subtitle: Check node logic to ensure types match, functions exist, and constraints hold.
scenes.label: Scene 6 / 9
stepSubtitles: Semantic Pass 1: Checking that variable 'x' is defined and in scope.
Semantic Pass 2: Verifying type constraints and assignments match.
Semantic Pass 3: Checking that the target function signature matches the call.
Type Checker detects violation: Attempting to assign string 'hello' to integer 'x'.

scenes.title: Optimization
scenes.subtitle: Produce faster and smaller code without changing program behavior.
scenes.label: Scene 7 / 9
stepSubtitles: Constant Folding: Optimizer pre-calculates expression '2 + 3' into constant '5' at compile time.
Dead Code Elimination: Discarding code branch 'if (false)' since it can never execute.

scenes.title: Code Generation
scenes.subtitle: Translate the optimized structure into processor instructions and binary bytes.
scenes.label: Scene 8 / 9
stepSubtitles: CodeGen: Emitting instruction to load value 2 into register R1.
CodeGen: Emitting instruction to load value 3 into register R2.
CodeGen: Emitting instruction to perform addition: R3 = R1 + R2.
CodeGen: Emitting instruction to store result from R3 to memory.
Translating assembly instructions into processor machine binary bytes...
Binary machine code is packed into the final target executable file.
